notes: Substrate task. Primary speed uses Vis .duration-ms, not shell wall time.

prompt: Find the exact Vis model path that should be used for Anthropic Opus 4.7 in this repo. Do not edit files. Answer with only the provider/model string.
notes: Real repo inspection. Expected answer comes from provider status/auth reality.

prompt: In this repo, identify the JSON field from `bin/vis run --json` that reports Vis internal task duration. Do not edit files. Answer with exactly one jq expression that returns task seconds from result.json.
notes: Real benchmark-harness task. Ensures agents know to use internal duration instead of CLI wall time.

prompt: Find the preferred structural Clojure editing API documented for Vis agents in this repo. Do not edit files. Answer with only the function name including namespace alias.
notes: Real repo task for structural editing knowledge.

prompt: Implement the remaining Vis prompt/context contract cleanup so normal tasks use less context without weakening proof. Keep XML-tagged prompt surfaces stable; keep `<journal>` as observed block evidence only; keep LLM-only reasoning out of journal and expose prior reasoning only through ITERATION_PREVIOUS_REASONING; keep skills as compact activation catalog plus full active-skill bodies only when loaded; keep preview/full value boundaries clear; and add tests that trivial/no-tool turns have substantially lower model-facing context while coding/proof tasks still expose provenance, intents, gates, audit, and tool evidence. Preserve Vis intent/proof/audit semantics and report exact token/context impact.
notes: Self-contained context-usage task distilled from TASKS/FOCUS. Optimize prompt/context without hiding proof-critical evidence.

prompt: Create a coherent Vis presentation/render contract. Extract shared internal presentation data shapes/rendering helpers for Markdown blocks, tool calls, system calls, provider errors, audit/attestation reports, details blocks, and Mermaid fenced blocks. Fix TUI rendering so Markdown inside details/tool details is rendered correctly instead of as plain text. Render audit/proof data as event/bundle/attestation/gate/plan/intent report with collapsed summary and optional Mermaid graph. Add regression tests for TUI details rendering, tool/system/provider-error presentation, Mermaid fenced rendering, and audit/proof collapsed/expanded output. Preserve headless terminal safety and proof/intents/audit semantics.
notes: Backlog presentation cluster. No workspace work. Judge compares render tests, screenshots/text snapshots if present, and proof/audit visibility.

prompt: Implement Extension Contract v2 in Vis. Add validated extension slots for `:ext/config`, `:ext/toggles`, `:ext/renderers`, `:ext/backgrounds`, lifecycle/event hooks including agent-end, and custom provider descriptors. Add public facade helpers for config/toggle reads and writes. Update settings/provider extension surfaces enough to prove the contract works headlessly; UI polish may be minimal but tested. Preserve extension aggregate as mutable state, not proof evidence; proof lifecycle hooks must carry structured payloads and never rely on scraping prompt prose. Add tests for spec validation, registration, toggles/config persistence, renderer dispatch, hook failure isolation, and custom provider descriptor validation.
notes: Backlog extension-v2 cluster. Does not include native workspaces.

prompt:
Improve the existing Vis Clojure language extension (`extensions/languages/clojure`) so agents can operate on real Clojure projects without shell guesswork. Add tested `z/` helpers for: discovering Clojure project/deps.edn aliases and source paths; selecting/running tests through clojure.test and lazytest when present; mapping a source namespace/file to its expected test namespace/file; computing classpath/dependency information through the Clojure CLI; finding which dependency/JAR provides a namespace or class; listing/inspecting JAR entries; and reading source/resource files from the effective classpath. Keep APIs headless-safe and bounded, avoid loading graphical UI code, and preserve Vis proof/intents/audit semantics. Add regression tests using small fixtures that cover clojure.test, lazytest detection, deps.edn aliases, dependency lookup, JAR/source inspection, and failure reporting. Verify with targeted tests plus ./verify.sh --quick. Report exact files changed, commands run, and proof/provenance implications.

Backbone already landed and must be treated as baseline (do not depend on PROOF.md): Vis intent lifecycle has statuses :suggested, :deferred, :active, :fulfilled, :abandoned; intent sources/owner_extension_id/parent_intent_id exist; acceptance/resume actors exclude :extension; defer trigger kinds are :defer/user-input, :defer/time, :defer/extension-signal, :defer/intent; defer sibling policy is :defer/continue-siblings or :defer/block-parent; abandonment scopes are :abandon/current-intent, :abandon/current-branch, :abandon/all-running; conversation_intent_cursor enforces one active running cursor; query APIs exist: db-list-intents, db-get-intent, db-intent-tree, db-deferred-intent-report; transition APIs exist: db-suggest-intent!, db-accept-intent!, db-defer-intent!, db-mark-intent-resumable!, db-resume-intent!, db-set-intent-cursor!, db-abandon-intent-with-scope!; each lifecycle transition must remain ledger-backed by provenance_event -> evidence_bundle -> attestation -> audit. For Clojure tooling, extension-created follow-up work may be suggested/deferred with owner_extension_id but the extension must never silently accept or resume its own intent. Tests must preserve this boundary and prove Clojure tooling failures are reported honestly as evidence/blockers, not fake success.
notes: Self-contained hard real Vis improvement task before Python work. It includes the intent/deferred-intent backbone from completed proof tasks 28-34 so agents must not read PROOF.md. Pi and Vis both attempt the same Vis Clojure-extension implementation in isolated worktrees; Opus judges diffs, tests, logs, speed, context, tool reliability, and proof honesty. This is not safe for the simple exact-answer runner yet.

prompt: Implement a first-class Vis Python language extension with working structured editing. Add an extension under extensions/languages/python that registers Python project guidance and exposes Python-aware structural edit helpers (parse/discover symbols/apply targeted edits) suitable for agent use. Prefer a robust existing parser available from the runtime or a small dependency only if justified. Add tests and docs. The extension must not load graphical UI code, must be headless-safe, and must preserve Vis proof/intents/audit semantics. Verify with targeted tests plus ./verify.sh --quick. Report exact files changed, commands run, and proof/provenance implications.
notes: Hard real Vis improvement task. Must run in paired clean worktrees; judge sees diff, tests, logs, and task metrics. This is not safe for the simple exact-answer runner yet.

prompt: Implement a simple playable Snake game in Python in the provided empty fixture repo. Requirements: use only the Python standard library, prefer curses when available with a deterministic non-interactive fallback for CI, include a clear game loop, snake movement, food spawning, growth, wall/self collision, score display, keyboard controls, and clean exit. Add tests for pure game-state logic. Keep implementation small and readable. Report how to run it and what checks passed.
notes: Pi vs Vis real coding competition. Vis should benefit from PYEXT1 Python structural editing. Judge compares final diffs and runnable behavior, not vibes.

prompt: Implement single-repository Git checkpoint/time-travel support for Vis without native workspace-manager scope. Capture tracked, staged, and bounded untracked files around turn start and mutating edit tools using safe Git refs such as `refs/vis/checkpoints/<conversation-id>/<turn-id>/<sequence>`. Do not mutate the user's index. Add before-restore checkpointing, safe restore that never deletes unrelated pre-existing untracked files, restore modes for files only / conversation only / files + conversation where existing conversation APIs support it, branch-from-checkpoint, and `v/checkpoints`, `v/checkpoint`, `v/checkpoint-restore!`, `v/checkpoint-branch!` symbols. Link checkpoints to provenance events. Explicitly exclude native workspace manager, submodule, attached-repo, and multirepo materialization work because another owner handles that.
notes: Single-repo checkpoint task only. All workspace/multirepo/submodule/attached-repo management is out of scope by user request.

prompt: Add Vis background-process and notification support. Add preconditioned patch/write safety where needed (sha/mtime for mutating patch paths), a background process lifecycle module with start/status/stop/tail symbols, bounded process output, cleanup on shutdown/conversation disposal, and notification adapters behind `vis/notify!` including OSC 777 terminal notifications. Add an agent-end lifecycle hook for 'agent response ended and waiting for input' and a formatter based on the final assistant answer. Include a settings/toggle path if extension config v2 exists; otherwise keep a small headless config path. Add regression tests for lifecycle cleanup, bounded output, notification formatting, hook dispatch, and no Ctrl+Y binding.
notes: Tooling/background/agent-end notification cluster. No workspace work.

prompt: Improve Vis provider behavior and visibility. Normalize provider trace metadata per iteration, ensure provider errors render as structured diagnostics with recovery steps, add toggles or settings for provider plans/system intents only if runtime data proves they are hidden by Vis, and implement custom OpenAI-compatible provider config with URL/key/model support. Add tests for provider-qualified model routing, provider error presentation, custom provider config coercion/persistence, and no mutation of active provider config during one-shot model override.
notes: Provider behavior/custom-provider cluster. Keeps model/provider failure observable for benchmark fairness.

prompt: Clean up Vis project guidance, docs, and general UX. Audit keybindings while keeping Ctrl+Y unbound, keep root README tiny and move long docs to mdBook, design a compact extension-managed project-guidance replacement for huge AGENTS.md while preserving compatibility for agent harnesses that read AGENTS.md, and document any ACP design spike only after extension/presentation seams are stable. Add tests or static checks for keybinding constraints and README stub size where practical.
notes: General UX/docs/guidance cluster. Lower priority, but kept in autoresearch as a real task.
